title: chassalia coriacea var. johnstonii
intro: Chassalia coriacea var. johnstonii, also known as Johnston's chassalia, is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical forests of Madagascar and other parts of the Indian Ocean. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Chassalia coriacea var. johnstonii include reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a herbal tea or infusion by steeping its leaves and stems in hot water, or as a decoction by boiling its roots in water, to harness its medicinal properties.
